expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude has no knowledge of the Docker container isolation pattern, container-setup-guide.md, or the host/container responsibility split. It would attempt to run apply directly in the current shell — without any Docker isolation, volume mounts, or container credentials.

Naive Claude might know to run opsx-verify after opsx-apply if it has general knowledge of the OpenSpec workflow. However, it would run both on the host without the container boundary, missing the isolation purpose.

Without the skill's loop protocol, naive Claude would attempt apply→verify once and report the result. There is no reason it would independently decide to loop up to 5 times or know about the iteration cap or the WARNING-vs-CRITICAL distinction that determines whether to continue.

Without the auto-archive step in the skill, naive Claude would report the verify result and stop. It would not automatically invoke opsx-archive, commit changes, or sync GitHub — it would require explicit user instruction for each subsequent step.

notes: Without the skill, the Docker container isolation pattern, loop iteration logic, and auto-archive chain are completely absent. Naive Claude might sequentially run apply then verify once, but the orchestration value of the skill (isolation, loop, auto-archive) requires the skill's explicit protocol files.
